proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is heavily concentrated in Bull regime (91.53%) and UP_TRI signal (98.3% of open positions); if regime shifts back to Bear this cohort would activate into an already UP_TRI-saturated book, so trader should monitor rebalancing when Bear re-emerges
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review per project_anchor §7 — Day-6 forced-exit dominance still unresolved
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.8pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 3), so the cohort is dormant and the flagged 91.53% Bull concentration is not compounded by a Bear-gated promotion. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-30) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is heavily concentrated in Bull regime (91.53%) and UP_TRI signal type (58/59); if regime shifts back to Bear rapidly, Bear-gated cohorts will activate against a portfolio poorly positioned for that regime
R-multiple 0.98 remains near Tier S threshold and is subject to D-8 Day-6 forced-exit review
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +30.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +30.8pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 3), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 and the flagged 91.53% concentration is on Bull, so a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion does not compound the concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 06-30) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Bull regime (91.53%), UP_TRI signal (98.3%), UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell (20.34%) (91.5%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Bull regime (91.53%), UP_TRI signal (98.3%), UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell (20.34%); recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and specifically block additions to sector=Bank until Bank cell drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits. Consider a Nifty/BankNifty short hedge sized to ~20-30% of book notional to offset the Bull-regime monoculture until cohort_health promotes Bull×UP_TRI beyond Candidate tier.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Bull→Choppy or Bull→Bear regime flip would unwind 54 of 59 positions simultaneously with no diversifying short/proxy exposure. The Bank cluster (12 positions) would compound the drawdown on any banking-sector-specific shock (RBI policy, NPA news), and with only n=4 cohort observations there is no statistical basis for the current sizing.

evidence: regime_concentration triggered: Bull at 0.9153 vs 0.80 threshold (54/59 positions)
UP_TRI signal share at 58/59 = 98.3%, above 0.85 monoculture threshold
UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell at 20.34% (12/59), breaches 0.20 cell threshold
Three additional concentrated cells: Energy 15.25%, Infra 13.56%, Other 13.56%
All four cells match Bull×UP_TRI cohort at Candidate tier only (n=4) — unvalidated edge
Book is effectively 100% directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 59/59)
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.concentration_value: Bull regime (91.53%), UP_TRI signal (98.3%), UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell (20.34%)
decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and specifically block additions to sector=Bank until Bank cell drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits. Consider a Nifty/BankNifty short hedge sized to ~20-30% of book notional to offset the Bull-regime monoculture until cohort_health promotes Bull×UP_TRI beyond Candidate tier.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Bull→Choppy or Bull→Bear regime flip would unwind 54 of 59 positions simultaneously with no diversifying short/proxy exposure. The Bank cluster (12 positions) would compound the drawdown on any banking-sector-specific shock (RBI policy, NPA news), and with only n=4 cohort observations there is no statistical basis for the current sizing.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 0.9153 (Bull, above the 0.80 threshold) AND the UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 20.34% (12 of 59 positions). UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 58/59 = 98.3% (above 0.85 threshold) with the book effectively a monoculture directional long. Matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=4) — unvalidated edge across all four concentrated cells (Bank 20.34%, Energy 15.25%, Infra 13.56%, Other 13.56%).
